relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After the rescue and refusal of corrupt wealth, the word of the Eternal came in vision: Do not fear. I am your shield and your reward.
Abram answered with the wound beneath the promise: What can you give me while I remain childless?
He named the ache plainly: no seed had been given, and his house would pass to another.
The Eternal answered that the heir would come from Abram’s own body.
Abram was brought outside to look at the stars, and the uncountable heavens became the image of his seed.
Abram trusted the Eternal, and that trust was reckoned as righteousness.
The Eternal reminded him: I brought you out to give you this land.
Abram asked how he would know the land would be possessed.
The Eternal called for animals, preparing a covenant not just spoken but cut.
Abram cut the animals and arranged the pieces, opening a ritual path for promise.
Birds of prey came down on the bodies, and Abram drove them away, guarding the covenant space.
As the sun descended, deep sleep fell on Abram, and a great darkness of awe and terror covered him.
The Eternal showed him that his seed would pass through sojourning, service, and affliction before return.
The oppressing nation would be judged, and the seed would come out with abundance.
Abram himself would go to his ancestors in peace, his own life completing in fullness.
The return would come in its appointed time, when the moral weight of the land had reached fullness.
In the darkness, fire and smoke passed between the pieces. The Divine presence moved through the cut place.
On that day the Eternal cut covenant with Abram, binding promise to seed and land.
The peoples of the land were named as the promise was given.
More peoples were named within the territory of promise.
The list completed the boundary of the promise, pointing toward a future still unfolding.
